reviews.review_msg: If you're weak at econ, taking it with Gordon Myers is a total nightmare. Lectures are boring and incomprehensible, the assigned textbook isn't all that helpful, and your mark is entirely made up of two midterms and a final exam. Make sure to buy the optional study guide, as that helps with studying for exams. He killed my interest in econ :(
great guy great lectures

Gordon himself was not a bad prof at all. He taught his lectures just like any other prof but it could make you fall asleep sometimes because he rambles about unrelated topics sometimes. The main issue I have is his marking scheme. He will not give partial marks at all. It's always a huge surprise when exam marks are released.

3 exams make up your mark. Exams and grading schemes are curved. Terrible lectures will put you to sleep. Not understanding and hard to get a marking error fixed. Don't bother with textbook and don't bother with lectures. Review his lecture slides and watch youtube videos. Tutorial questions are similar to exam questions. Need to go to tuts for ans

ECON103 is pretty straight forward with Myers. I would recommend skipping lectures and reading the textbook. GO TO YOUR TUTORIALS. 

To do well on exams, make sure you understand your tutorial questions, then do the end of chapter textbook questions. Just doing that for each chapter got me an A-.

Unless you buy the study guide, you probably won't do that well (unless you have A LOT of time to study). The class was based on three exams. Even if you think you understand the questions on the exams and you've got the right answers, you don't. His lectures aren't useful because he uses a lot of personal examples that don't help you understand.

Econ 103 is definitely a difficult course - with Myers - keep up with the lecture slides and tutorial questions each week. Exams were very similar to tutorial questions. Even though he's monotone, he's a very caring prof and has a lot of knowledge about economics. It can be difficult to follow him  but puts a lot of effort into the course.

Grab a blanket! myers rambles a lot about his own life+achievents in a slow monotone voice, if you did not get much sleep at night, you'll be sure to doze off in class. Classwise, he doesnt cover all the material so you must read the textbook on your own. He likes to reword the exam questions so make sure you understand content conceptually.

I did bad in this class because I have a difficult time with economics. I think prof Myers is ok, his lectures are not helpful at all. Try and utilize outside resources if you struggle with economics.

Took the class in spring 2021. Entirely test based with the grades coming from 3 midterms and the final. Great leactures and easy tests since the practice problems are identical to the test. Only major issue were some tech difficulties during the 3rd final midterm but the prof allowed students to choose if they wanted to void that midterm.

Gordon was a good prof. I didn't find his lectures that helpful. I relied on the textbook, his tutorial questions, and the textbook study guide to do well. His class is 100% test-based but his exams are very fair and actually easy if u understand the concepts instead of memorizing stuff.

Took the course because of his rating, but found that there was no additional benefit from the Office Hours that replaced the 2 hour lecture time on myschedule. Prof is encouraging and obviously cares that his students succeed. Office Hours are helpful if you have questions. Otherwise, take good notes of the text and use the practice questions.

Spring21 remote. Stopped listening to his pre-rec audio lectures, painful to listen to since he doesnt incl. slides with it. Youd have to play slides yourself & make sure the page youre at matches where hes at. Perhaps has no time to learn tech. TA Hannah Reid is excellent, the best TA Ive had. Only 3 tests & final, no asmt. Final was tough.

He is a fun professor, the class would have been better in person but I really enjoyed it. just study his tutorial questions and skim through the textbook for topics you don't understand. The applications in his lectures were very interesting!

Gordon's lessons are all prerecorded but for whatever reason, he only records his mic and doesn't record the PowerPoint with it so you kinda have to open the audio and slides separately and guess when he moves on to the next slide. He also decided to not curve the class at the end of the semester which was unfair since everyone was expecting a curv

You are basically teaching yourself in an online class with professor Myers. His classes are extremely unorganized and he goes on about things that have nothing to do with the class. I would highly recommend not taking this course with him.

Gordon is a great prof for this class. Lecture slides are straightforward, but the textbook helps if you need more context. I didn't buy the extra study guide. When we had a glitchy exam, he didn't blame us and gave individuals the choice to keep or discard our grade on it. Go to office hrs if you are worried about exams, he is reassuring.

Gordon did a poor job of switching to online class in the spring of 2020. He changed the grading scheme from being curved to not being curved at the very end of the semester, which was extremely unfair to students.

I really appreciated Professor Myers, everything he taught seemed so deliberate and carefully thought out, which made it palpable to understand in an introductory economics course. Taking his class while school moved online because of corona, he showed he cared by telling us that everything will be okay eventually. He seems like a really nice guy.

Honestly, his lectures are pretty bland, but if you care about learning about economics then he can be interesting. He reads off the slides and likes to drag explanations out. Also, his handwriting is horrible and was barely legible. His exams were pretty easy and some of the questions from the tutorials were there (which I recommend going to).

Gordon was a good prof. His exams consisted of 2 term tests and 1 final exam. His 1st term test was SUPER easy but his next two tests were quite hard-- he is open and encourages you to reach out for help if you need it though. Teaching wise he is good but can go fast someone. Overall good prof, does talk about Trump a little which is annoying.

Econ 103 is easy in itself, but Meyers made it even easier! The questions on the exams are the same (literal copy paste) of tutorial questions, and he'll tell you this so believe him. Aside from that, a little too much orange man bad talk in his lectures

He is a good teacher and cares about his students a lot! The class has been easy so far and exam material is exactly what is covered in tutorials and textbooks if you do the readings and tutorial questions. However, lectures cover the exact same notes from the textbook so it can be boring and does not give much incentive on coming to lectures.
His classes are really interactive and interesting...
He is a nice prof! The lectures those are very interesting and informative. For exams, memorize the ans to the tut Qs! His final is not hard as well.
His lectures are so good that I never even had to study or use my textbook.  Really good professor. A+
Honestly, the midterms were so easy that the practice questions on myeconlab were infinitely more difficult.

Gordon is an extremely boring prof; however, his exams are fair and if you need extra help he is willing to help. As long as you read the textbook and do the questions from the study guide you will do well on the final and on the midterm.

Lectures were not really mandatory as slides were all posted online. Textbook used for  clarification, but overall all necessary information were in his powerpoints. Fair tests as long as you studied.
You can definitely skip out of his lectures, it's useless. All you have to do is read the textbook, but the final is extremely hard!

The textbook is extremely helpful Midterms were easy Final was a little harder but if u study a bit more u will do good! tutorials and lectures are not really useful unless u read the chapter before hand.

Awful awful professor. He doesn't do any examples in class; he opens up each chapter from the textbook; blabs for an hour and then powers through all the slides while reading things off of the screen. You can do really well by reading the textbook and understanding the concepts. Tutorial questions and study guide are essential. Classes are boring.

Gordon is a great guy. Yes, his slides are straight out of the textbook, but he uses very thorough explanations and interesting examples. He really wants his students to understand what he is talking about. He's always open for questions, I spent a lot of time in his office hours. Exams are difficult but fair.

Lectures were not necessary. All the material covered is straight from the text, which he provides on powerpoints available online. Textbook is useful for further clarification. Exams were fair as long as you study. Overall, a good prof. Recommended.

Prof is good. He covers all the material clearly. For people who aren't interested in Economics he will be boring, but I found him to be very interesting and insightful. He also had very fair exams if you studied.
Not boring but also not very useful
His lectures are sleeping pills! So boring. Just take notes on the textbook and do the questions in the study guide and you should do fine. Exams are fair.
OMG....must be the boring-est prof everr. he doesn't give you a break for the 2 hour class and he makes the content sooo boring and dry

Lectures are killer. Especially the 2 hour one. You will fall asleep guaranteed. All of his lectures (posted on  Webct) are straight from the textbook. Monotone voice. Study guide & textbook are really helpful. Go to tutorials. Midterms were fair but final was horrible.
Prof is nice, patient and easy-going. But content is dry and boring. Some people fall asleep in that class. Tests are fair as long as you read the book and everything.

Go to lecture and take good notes. Some tricky questions on the exams to see who's been paying more attention. Barely used the text in the exams but it helps to understand the material. Not much reading. Overall decent prof.

pretty good prof. he takes things slow, makes sure that you understand the material fully. love his 2 midterm system! one thing: ALWAYS GO TO LECTURE AND TUTORIAL. for some reason he loves the text book even though its a bus. text. he can be boring and sometimes his lectures does not make sense until you read the text. would take him again.

Not a good prof. He's nice but does not know what he's talking about. Always has to refer to his notes and he's teaches things that aren't in the textbook. So you have to know both textbook stuff and stuff he teaches. TAKE 290 WITH SOMEONE ELSE !
Nice prof. Know his stuff well and give time for you to think through the content in class. I like his scale system.

Fill in the blanks online notes and covers mostly the same thing as the textbook! His lectures are very slow and clear, in fact the speed at which he was moving was boring me to tears!. I believe this is why students do poorly, the way he teaches it is very dry. He had to scale up both midterms and the final exam even though the material was easy!
easy and clear prof.  fair tests & easy final.  take this prof!!!!
everything in the lecture is rite out of the book, no extra info

hardd class! just got my first midterm back and the average mark is 40%!! hard exam but he added marks to everyones exam to bring up the average.. hopefully next exam will be easier.. the best thing to do to prepare for the exams is go over tutorial questions and do practise questions at the end of the chapter..
attend the lectures those are very interesting and informative. For exams, memorize the ans to the tut Qs and u r going to be okie, 100% guarenteed!
No Comments
Very easy lecture...nice professor
No Comments
Just read over his notes, no textbook require!
No Comments
No Comments
No Comments
